title: leucanthemum subalpinum
intro: Leucanthemum subalpinum, also known as alpine daisy or white daisy, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. Leucanthemum subalpinum is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
